entities.name: ACACIA ALBIDA
entities.description: A tree species studied for its effects on the growth and yield of cereal crops

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop evaluated for yield and growth in relation to distance from Acacia albida

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop evaluated for yield and growth in relation to distance from Acacia albida

entities.name: TEFF
entities.description: A cereal crop evaluated for yield and growth in relation to distance from Acacia albida

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the crops, measured as a response to tree distance and shading

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The developmental progress of the cereal crops

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted

entities.name: DISTANCE FROM TREES
entities.description: The radial distance from Acacia albida trees, ranging from 1.5m to 12.5m

entities.name: SHADING EFFECT
entities.description: The reduction of sunlight reaching crops due to the canopy of Acacia albida

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: Soil components that may contribute to increased crop yields near tree canopies

entities.name: SOIL NUTRIENTS
entities.description: Nutrients in the soil that may contribute to increased crop yields near tree canopies

entities.name: LOPPING
entities.description: The practice of pruning tree branches to reduce shading effects before sowing

entities.name: COMPATIBILITY
entities.description: The degree to which a crop can be successfully integrated with Acacia albida under shade conditions
